preamble: The vowel [e] shifts to [a] through lowering and backing, a vowel shift pattern attested in various language families. In Chukchi-Kamchatkan languages, this represents a synchronic vowel harmony alternation where mid front vowel /e₂/ alternates with low back vowel /a/ depending on the presence of 'dominant' vowels (back/lower vowels) in the word.

examples.note: Chukchi vowel harmony: 'recessive' vowel /e₂/ lowers and backs to [a] when a 'dominant' vowel (back or lower vowels /a, o, e₂/ or /u/) appears in the word. Height-based harmony system where dominants override recessives. This is a synchronic process, not historical change, maintaining allophonic variation within paradigms.
Historical lowering of long mid front vowels in certain phonological environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
